---
title: "Tree Tools R Package"
output: pdf_document
date: "2026-04-15"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


Goal 2: Detect Cross Dating Errors 

```{r}
#' @param data A tidy tree ring data frame containing columns for 'year', 'sample_id', and 'ring_width'.
#' @return A summary table of flagged samples with cross dating errors and the specific reason they were flagged.
#' @export
# To work: needs a tidy data fram with columns year, sample_id, and ring_width created by goal 1
# Column names can change depending on goal 1 implementation

detect_crossdate_errors <- function(data) {
  
  # Check for negative widths
  physical_errors <- data %>%
    dplyr::filter(ring_width < 0) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(issue = "Negative growth detected")

  # Check for missing rings/zeros
  missing_rings <- data %>%
    dplyr::filter(ring_width == 0) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(issue = "Potential missing ring (zero width)")

  # Statistical outlier check (width > 3 SD)
  outliers <- data %>%
    dplyr::group_by(sample_id) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(z_score = (ring_width - mean(ring_width)) / sd(ring_width)) %>%
    dplyr::filter(abs(z_score) > 3) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(issue = "Statistical outlier (Z > 3)")

  # Combine all checks into one summary report
  flagged_summary <- dplyr::bind_rows(physical_errors, missing_rings, outliers)
  return(flagged_summary)}
```
